title: Sanger Tree of Life HMW DNA Extraction: Automated Nucleated Blood Nanobind
author: Pacific Biosciences
materials: Thermo Fisher KingFisher™ 1 mL 96-well deep-well plate
In one Thermo Fisher KingFisher™ 1 mL 96-well deep-well plate, labelled Lysis Plate, add the following reagents into each well for the number of samples being processed:

steps.title: Set up the six remaining Thermo...
steps.instruction: Set up the six remaining Thermo Fisher KingFisher™ 1 mL 96-well deep-well plates required for the protocol as detailed in the table below. Ensure that the reagents are added into the wells corresponding to those in which samples were added into the Lysis Plate: Plate Reagent(s) required per well 1 Tip Plate 96-well tip comb (no reagent) 2 Nanobind Disc Storage Plate One 3 mm Nanobind disc 3 Wash Plate 1 700 µL Buffer CW1 4 Wash Plate 2 700 µL Buffer CW2 5 Wash Plate 3 700 µL Buffer CW2 6 Elution Plate 100 µL Buffer EB

steps.title: Select the required DNA extraction protocol...
steps.instruction: Select the required DNA extraction protocol in the protocol list on the KingFisher™ Apex (details below in KingFisher™ Apex DNA Extraction Protocol section/attached file) and select using the play button.

steps.title: Load the filled plates onto the...
steps.instruction: Load the filled plates onto the instrument following the instructions provided on screen. Once the final plate is loaded, the protocol will automatically begin; this takes approximately 75 minutes.

steps.title: Approximately 22 minutes into the protocol,...
steps.instruction: Approximately 22 minutes into the protocol, the instrument will prompt the user to remove the Lysis Plate from the instrument and add 300 μL of isopropanol to each well containing a sample. Ensure that the isopropanol is added gently against the side of the well rather than directly into the lysis solution, as this may affect extraction purity. Once this has been done, re-insert the plate into the instrument and press ‘Next’ to resume the protocol.

steps.title: Once the protocol has completed, follow...
steps.instruction: Once the protocol has completed, follow the on-screen instructions to remove plates from the instrument.

steps.title: Using a standard P200 pipette and...
steps.instruction: Using a standard P200 pipette and 200 μL pipette tips, transfer eluates from the Elution Plate to microcentrifuge tubes for storage.

steps.title: Pipette mix the eluate 10 times...
steps.instruction: Pipette mix the eluate 10 times with a standard P200 pipette and 200 μL pipette tip to homogenise the DNA within the sample and disrupt any viscous regions.

steps.title: Allow the eluates to incubate at...
steps.instruction: Allow the eluates to incubate at room temperature overnight to allow the DNA to solubilise.

steps.title: Following the overnight rest, pipette mix...
steps.instruction: Following the overnight rest, pipette mix 10 times with a standard P200 pipette and 200 μL pipette tip, then perform the required QC.

steps.title: Add 5 to 10 μL of nucleated blood
steps.instruction: Add 5 to 10 μL of nucleated blood.

steps.title: Add 150 μL of Buffer BL3. Note
steps.instruction: Add 150 μL of Buffer BL3. Note: add this reagent gently against the side of the well, as adding it directly into the lysis solution may affect the extraction performance.
